parsed.answer: SOS originally stood for nothing — it was not an acronym. It was chosen at the 1906 International Radiotelegraph Convention in Berlin purely for the simplicity and distinctiveness of its Morse code pattern (· · · — — — · · ·): three dots, three dashes, three dots. The popular backronyms 'Save Our Souls' and 'Save Our Ship' were invented after the signal was already in use.
